title: Crispy Floral Algerian Potato Flatbread
description: A fragrant, herbinfused potato flatbread that captures the nostalgic flavors of Algeria with a floral twist of dried lavender and fresh herbs.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F) and grease a baking sheet with a tablespoon of olive oil.
Steam the peeled potatoes until tender, about 15 minutes; mash until smooth and let cool slightly.
In a small bowl, whisk together the crushed lavender, rosemary, cumin, coriander, paprika, saffron water, salt, pepper, and lemon zest.
Fold the spice mixture into the mashed potatoes until well combined.
Stir in the beaten egg and the remaining olive oil; if making a raw‑friendly version, skip the egg and add a little extra potato or a plant‑based binder.
Transfer the mixture onto the prepared sheet and press it into a 1‑inch thick flatbread, about 30 cm across.
Bake for 18–20 minutes, or until the edges are golden and the surface is crisp and slightly blistered.
Remove from oven, let rest 5 minutes, then slice into wedges. Garnish with edible flowers if desired and serve warm.
